common.searchHint: Search by page title, topic, or path. Use arrow keys to choose, Enter to open.
common.searchTryDifferent: Try shorter queries, synonyms, or different words.

home.heroSubtitle: An open source education hub for Zcash
home.heroDescription: Learn about Zcash privacy technology, explore interactive visualizers, and contribute to the ecosystem
home.description: ZecHub is the community-driven education hub for the Zcash cryptocurrency (ZEC). Zcash is a digital currency providing censorship resistant, secure & private payments. The Zcash Blockchain utilises highly advanced 'verifiable' zk-snarks that do not require Trusted Setup following the NU5 network upgrade in 2022.

startHere.title: Start Here
startHere.content: Find out what Zcash is and ZEC use cases

pickWallet.title: Pick a Wallet
pickWallet.content: Wallets allow you to receive and spend Zcash privately. Some also support encrypted memos

resources.title: Zcash Resources
resources.content: Here are guides that help users understand Zcash Full Nodes, zk-SNARKs and more

whatIsZcash.title: What is Zcash?
whatIsZcash.description: It is an open-source, blockchain ledger that features a sophisticated zero-knowledge proving system. It specializes in offering a higher standard of privacy through its proving system preserving confidentiality of transaction metadata. At its core, Zcash is private data ownership that is permissionlessly transferred when transactions are made.

zcashMe.title: Zcash.me
zcashMe.description: Zcash.Me is a public directory of Zcash users featuring private messaging, leaderboards, and verified users. Connect, explore, and interact with the Zcash community.

globalAmbassadors.title: Zcash Global Ambassadors
globalAmbassadors.description: Zcash Global Ambassadors are community leaders dedicated to promoting privacy-focused cryptocurrency adoption and education worldwide. Each ambassador project focuses on building awareness and engagement within their respective regions.

newsletter.title: Shielded Newsletter
newsletter.description: Subscribe using your Unified Address to get shielded access to Zcash Ecosystem Updates & Network Stats direct to your wallet!

free2z.title: Free2Z
free2z.description: Free2Z is a social platform powered by Zcash. With peer-to-peer donations, a revenue sharing program, advanced creative tools and a massive online global community.

payWithZcash.title: Pay with Zcash
payWithZcash.description: This website is an answer to the question: Where can I pay with Zcash? The directory is free to use. The items listed are for informational purposes only, and not endorsements of any kind. Enjoy!

footer.description: An open source education hub for Zcash

visualizer.title: Zcash Visualizers
visualizer.description: Interactive educational tools to understand Zcash privacy technology, infrastructure, and zero-knowledge proofs
visualizer.basicDescription: Foundational concepts and essential features of Zcash
visualizer.advancedDescription: Deep technical dives into cryptography, consensus, and infrastructure
visualizer.contributorsDescription: Ways to contribute to the Zcash ecosystem and earn rewards
visualizer.autoPlayNote: Each visualizer runs automatically. Use controls to navigate or pause.

pools.title: Value Pools & Address Types
pools.description: Explore Zcash privacy pools and address types

payWith.title: Pay with Zcash
payWith.description: Discover where and how to use ZEC for private payments

dex.title: Zcash Exchanges (DEX)
dex.description: Permissionless, censorship-resistant access to ZEC using decentralized exchanges

consensus.title: Consensus
consensus.description: How do hundreds of nodes agree on chain state?

miningHalo.title: Zcash Mining
miningHalo.description: Understanding Equihash mining and recursive zero-knowledge proofs

privacyUseCases.title: Privacy Use Cases
privacyUseCases.description: Real-world applications of privacy technology on Zcash

governance.title: Governance & Dev Fund
governance.description: Community-driven development and decentralized decision making

bounties.title: ZecHub Bounties
bounties.description: Contribute to ZecHub and earn ZEC

daoProposal.title: DAO Proposals
daoProposal.description: Step-by-step guide to creating a ZecHub DAO governance proposal

coinholderGrants.title: Coinholder Directed Grants
coinholderGrants.description: Retroactive funding directed by ZEC holders

openSource.title: Open Source Repositories
openSource.description: Contribute to Zcash open source projects

wallets.title: Zcash Wallets
wallets.description: Choose a wallet that fits your needs

tutorials.title: Tutorials
tutorials.description: Learn by doing with hands-on tutorials

dao.title: ZecHub DAO
dao.headerDescription: An open source educational platform where community members collaborate on creating, validating, and promoting content that supports the Zcash ecosystem
beliefs: Education should be open-source and accessible worldwide
Community members have a right to earn ZEC privately

features.title: First Zcash Ecosystem DAO
features.description: ZecHub is pioneering decentralized governance in the Zcash community, with funds held primarily within the Zcash shielded pool for maximum privacy.

features.title: Global Collaboration
features.description: Global Ambassadors, Community Grants members, and community experts unite to guide the direction of Zcash and Privacy Technology education.

features.title: SubDAO Framework
features.description: Any group can create SubDAOs to form projects, create proposals, or request Retroactive Compensation via the DAO DAO module.

dao.governanceDescription: All DAO proposals are public and transparent. Anyone can create a proposal to vote on. In support of community transparency, ZecHub DAO posts all governance proposals in the Zcash Community Forum ZecHub Governance thread.

newsletter.title: Newsletter
newsletter.description: Stay updated with the latest Zcash news

charts.headerSubtitle: Analyze Zcash network metrics and trends

hackathon.title: ZecHub Hackathon
hackathon.heroIntro: Build across five tracks, from infra to games, then document and demo your work. Official kickoff
hackathon.endedNote: This hackathon window has ended, thanks to everyone who took part.
hackathon.tracksIntro: Align your project with a track for clarity when you submit, judges and the community use them to browse entries.

infrastructure.name: Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: ZSA GUI, Node Launcher, Zallet web dashboard etc.

games.name: Games
games.description: Wiki Article game similar to catfishing.net, ZEC Poker, Battleship etc.

frost.name: FROST
frost.description: Threshold signing wallets, Shielded ZEC Escrow, Social Recovery

zcashLogin.name: Zcash Login
zcashLogin.description: Auth and identity flows that use Zcash primitives for sign-in or access.

accounting.name: Accounting
accounting.description: Reporting, workflows for teams handling ZEC, Payment management system.

hackathon.prizePoolDesc: Build, submit, and compete for the community prize pool.

quickStart.title: ZecHub Developer Quick Start
quickStart.description: Find the essential tools, references, and setup steps for building on Zcash.

hackathon.rulesDesc: Keep submissions fair and easy to judge.

rules.mainnet: Projects must interact with the Zcash mainnet network.
rules.oneProject: Teams can submit only one final project per team.
rules.docs: All submissions must include clear setup and usage documentation.
rules.respect: Respect privacy, security, and community guidelines in both code and content.

hackathon.faqDesc: Quick answers before you dive in.

whoCanParticipate.question: Who can participate?
whoCanParticipate.answer: Anyone can join: developers, designers and multidisciplinary teams.

validSubmission.question: What must a valid submission include?
validSubmission.answer: A working prototype, video demo, basic documentation, and an explanation of how it uses the Zcash network.

whereSubmit.question: Where do we submit projects?
whereSubmit.answer: Share your video demo in the Zcash Global Discord and include any relevant repository or demo links.

winnersSelected.question: How are winners selected?
winnersSelected.answer: A public community process is run via ZecHub DAO channels after submissions close.

whatTracks.question: What are the tracks?
whatTracks.answer: This hackathon's themes are Infrastructure, Games, FROST, Zcash Login, and Accounting. Pick the track that best matches your build, you can still submit a project not defined within tracks.

hackathon.submitIntro: Follow these five steps to get your project from idea to official entry.

build.title: Build your project
build.description: Create a working prototype that interacts with the Zcash mainnet. Pick a track and keep a development log as you go.

record.title: Record a video demo
record.description: Capture a short walkthrough showing your project in action. Clearly explain what it does and how it uses the Zcash network.

document.title: Document your work
document.description: Write a README with setup instructions, usage notes, and your open-source license. Push everything to a public GitHub repo.

upload.title: Upload your code
upload.descSuffix: on GitHub so the community can review your work.

share.title: Share in Discord
share.description: Post your video demo in the Zcash Global Discord with your repo and demo links before July 15, 2026 (UTC).

hackathon.deadlinePrefix: All submissions must be posted in Discord by
hackathon.deadlineSuffix: . Winners are selected via a public community vote in ZecHub DAO channels shortly after submissions close.
hackathon.previousIntro: Browse earlier ZecHub hackathon builds by edition. Each project card links directly to its GitHub folder for code, notes, and demos where available.
hackathon.errorCouldNotLoad: Could not load live project list
hackathon.errorShowingSaved: Showing the saved previous hackathon project list instead.
hackathon.noProjects: No project folders were returned. Open the repo tree to verify structure.
hackathon.needHelpDesc: The Zcash Global Discord is the best place for build questions, infra tips, and hackathon chatter.

popup.subtitle: Build privacy-first applications on Zcash. Compete for prizes, earn ZEC, and shape the future of financial freedom.

developers.title: Zcash Developer Resources
developers.cardSectionTitle: How would you like to get started?

cards.learnContent: Explore the official documentation and learn the basics of Zcash technology.
cards.tutorialsContent: Follow step-by-step tutorials to build on Zcash, from creating wallets to integrating Zcash into your applications.
cards.quickStartContent: Get up and running with Zcash development quickly. Learn installation, configuration, and basic operations.

introLinks.introToZcashDesc: Learn about Zcash, its history, and its core principles.
introLinks.introToZecDesc: Discover the use cases and benefits of ZEC, Zcash native cryptocurrency.
introLinks.introToZebraDesc: Understanding how Zebra powers the Zcash network with a modular and secure Rust-based implementation
introLinks.introToLightwalletdDesc: Understanding the lightwalletd project—a scalable server written in Go that efficiently serves compact blockchain data to Zcash light clients via gRPC.
introLinks.zalletBookDesc: Introduction to Zallet, a full-node Zcash wallet written in Rust and designed as a replacement for the zcashd wallet.
introLinks.developerResourcesDesc: Links to Zcash further Technical Documentation.

fundamentalsLinks.addressEncodingDesc: Learn how Zcash addresses and keys are encoded.
fundamentalsLinks.transactionsDesc: Understand how transactions work in Zcash.
fundamentalsLinks.feesDesc: Learn about transaction fees and how they are calculated.
fundamentalsLinks.nodesZcashdDesc: Set up and manage Zcash nodes using zcashd.
fundamentalsLinks.nodesZebradDesc: Explore Zebra, an alternative Zcash node implementation.
fundamentalsLinks.lightwalletServersDesc: Learn about lightwalletd, a lightweight Zcash wallet server.
fundamentalsLinks.blockExplorersDesc: Explore Zcash blockchains using block explorers.
fundamentalsLinks.shadeAgentDesc: Learn about Shade Agents—the first truly autonomous AI agents on NEAR—powered by secure TEEs and decentralized Chain Signatures to manage assets, access LLMs, and execute multichain transactions independently.

advancedLinks.introToStackDesc: Understand the architecture and components of the Zcash stack.
advancedLinks.zecDevDesc: Here you will find projects, resources and tools created and maintained in the context of the (Zcash) Developer Relations Engineer grant. See also the
advancedLinks.introToZingolibDesc: Understanding the zingolib repository—a Rust-based API and CLI for interacting with zcashd via Lightwalletd.
advancedLinks.webZjsDesc: A JavaScript/TypeScript library for building Zcash-enabled web applications using WebAssembly.
advancedLinks.noirWalletSdkDesc: TypeScript SDK and example dApp for integrating Zcash applications with the Noir Wallet browser extension.
advancedLinks.zcashIkaDesc: Split-key custody for Zcash and multi-chain agents via 2PC-MPC. On-chain spend policy, privacy attestation, ZIP 244 transaction builder.
advancedLinks.developmentGuidelinesDesc: Follow best practices for developing on Zcash.
advancedLinks.arboristCallsDesc: The Zcash Arborist Calls are bi-weekly protocol development meetings focused on tracking upcoming protocol deployment logistics, consensus node implementation issues, and protocol research.

developers.feedbackHelpful: Thank you for your feedback! Make this page even better by answering a few questions. If you need help, you can reach out to the community on our
developers.feedbackNotHelpful: Sorry to hear that. Please let us know how we can improve this page. You can reach out to the community on our

developersQuickStart.heroSubtitle: Get up and running with Zcash development. Choose your stack and follow the guide.
developersQuickStart.noteDownloadsBlockchain: First run downloads the entire blockchain (~250 GB)
developersQuickStart.noteInitialSync: Initial sync can take several hours
developersQuickStart.noteKeepSecure: Keep your zebrad.toml secure and private
developersQuickStart.lightwalletdInfo: is a server that provides a lightweight interface to the Zcash blockchain, designed for mobile and desktop wallets that don't need to run a full node.
developersQuickStart.zainoInfo: is a Rust-based indexer for the Zcash blockchain. It serves both light clients (wallets that don't store the full history) and full clients / block explorers, providing access to finalized chain, the non-finalized best chain, and the mempool.
developersQuickStart.zingoCliInfo: is a command-line lightwalletd-proxy client built in Rust. Releases are currently provisional — this guide covers compiling from source.
developersQuickStart.zingoSyncWarning: ⚠ This will perform a full sync on first run, similar to lightwalletd.

governanceHowto.pageSubtitle: A comprehensive guide to participating in ZecHub DAO governance
governanceHowto.firstStepsDesc: Get set up to participate in DAO governance
governanceHowto.draftProposalDesc: Structure and prepare your proposal for submission
governanceHowto.installKeplrDesc: Set up your Keplr wallet to interact with the DAO
governanceHowto.acquireJunoDesc: Get JUNO tokens to interact with the daodao contract. Contact core-team members for assistance.
governanceHowto.viewOpenProposalsDesc: Check out current proposals and voting opportunities
governanceHowto.formatNote: Format: Proposals may be written in .Md or .txt format
governanceHowto.stageBackgroundDesc: Provide some history of the general topic / service
governanceHowto.stageWhyDesc: Present the driving reason for your proposal. May use bullet points
governanceHowto.stageProposalDesc: Clearly detail your proposal in full
governanceHowto.stageActionDesc: What happens upon successful vote - may provide timeline of actions
governanceHowto.getFeedbackDesc: Share your draft in the ZecHub #proposals channel on Discord for early feedback
governanceHowto.walkthroughDesc: Watch a full walkthrough of how to write and structure a proposal
governanceHowto.postInDiscordDesc: Share your final proposal in #proposals and tag @core-team
governanceHowto.submitOnPortalDesc: Use the official ZecHub voting site to create your proposal
governanceHowto.loginNote: ⚠️ You need to login with either Keplr or Keplr Mobile

sitemap.subheading: Complete overview of all pages and resources available on the Zechub Wiki
